explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is currently in 'PSVMode' (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode) and the 'stopVentilation' command is issued by the user, then the system must transition to 'StandbyMode' (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode) within the next 1 time unit. This ensures a prompt and defined transition to the Standby Mode upon the user stopping ventilation in PSV mode.

The property states that it is always the case that if the system is in PSV mode and the user stops the ventilation, then in the very next state, the system transitions to the StandbyMode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode). This directly captures the required conditional transition.

The property states that globally (G), if the user stops the ventilation (stopVentilation) and the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode) at the current moment, then in the very next moment (X), the system shall transition to Standby Mode (StandbyMode). This directly captures the required transition condition.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is currently in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode and the user initiates a stop ventilation command, then in the very next state, the system transitions to Standby mode (also known as VentilationOff mode). This captures the requirement for an immediate transition to the 'VentilationOff' state upon the user's action while in PSV mode.

The natural language statement describes a global requirement: whenever the user stops ventilation while the system is in PSV mode, the system must eventually transition to the VentilationOff state (StandbyMode). Template 19, 'G (bool_exp2 -> (F bool_exp3))', perfectly captures this 'if condition then eventually consequence' pattern. 'bool_exp2' is set to the trigger condition 'stopVentilation & PSVMode', and 'bool_exp3' is set to the desired consequent state 'StandbyMode' (which is equivalent to VentilationOff). Since the eventuality is unbounded, N_DURATION is set to null.

The property states that whenever the system is in PSV mode and the user stops ventilation, the system must transition to StandbyMode in the next state. This ensures that the specified transition occurs immediately upon the conjunction of the two conditions.

The property states that if the user stops the ventilation while the system is in PSV mode, then in the very next state, the system must transition to Standby mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff). This captures the immediate response to the user's action.

The property states that globally, if the user stops the ventilation while the system is in PSV mode, then eventually the system will enter Standby Mode, which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode. This captures the requirement for the transition to occur conditionally.

The property states that globally (G), if the user stops the ventilation (stopVentilation) while the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode), then in the next state (X), the system shall be in Standby mode (StandbyMode), which is equivalent to VentilationOff. This accurately captures the conditional transition from PSV to VentilationOff upon user action.

The property states that globally, if the user stops the ventilation (stopVentilation) while the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode), then in the next state, the system will be in Standby Mode (StandbyMode), which represents VentilationOff. This captures the conditional transition described in the natural language.
